evidence_direction_note: Cascade gate probe: narrowly tests whether Phase-1 + Phase-2 V_s runtime flags alone diversify action-class entropy relative to a fully-OFF baseline. A FAIL does not weaken MECH-269/287/288 substrate claims -- it indicates the MECH-284 Phase-3 consumer is required to convert the V_s observables into behaviour. Telemetry artefact identified 2026-04-24: boundary_event_count and broadcast_event_count are inflated ~N/2x because the probe adds len(_boundary_event_queue) / len(_broadcast_event_queue) each tick but nothing in the waking path drains either queue (agent.py:1013, 1039 only extend; tick_anchor_set consumes a local events list, not the queue). Under uniformly-distributed firing over T=1200 ticks the counter reads ~M*T/2, so seed 42's 28094 corresponds to ~47 true boundaries (~0.04/tick) and seed 7's 13403 to ~22 (~0.02/tick) -- both consistent with the two-scale segmenter honouring min_segment_length under monostrategy lock. broadcast_event_count == boundary_event_count in both seeds is the signature of the same accumulation bug (one broadcast per boundary, both queues grow in lockstep). The action_class_entropy metric is unaffected; the cascade-gate FAIL (Phase-3 required) verdict stands. EventSegmenter / InvalidationTrigger substrates are not over-firing.
